name: Hunter Events
description: The Hunter's search yields food, or trouble, or both. These events occur during journey travel when the Event Target falls to the Hunter. Distinct from dedicated Hunting Days, which use a separate mechanical flow.

fishing.label: Fishing
fishing.description: The hunter finds water worth fishing — stream, pool, lake edge.

events.label: A good fishing spot — a deep still pool, fish rising at dusk. A patient hour yields 1d3 meals of trout, perch, or salmon depending on the water.
events.notes: Positive event. Success yields rations; failure is lost time.

events.label: Thin pickings — the water is fished out, or the season wrong. A single small fish, or nothing.
events.notes: Minor event. Success yields 1 ration; failure, nothing.

events.label: Something disturbs the fishing — a shape in the water, a sudden silence of birds. The fish stop rising; something else is hunting here.
events.on_eye: Whatever disturbed the fishing now turns on the hunter; roll a predator.
events.notes: Cross-reference to Predators data file on Eye of Sauron.

hunting.label: Hunting
hunting.description: The hunter tracks prey through the undergrowth or across open country.

events.label: The hunt succeeds — rabbit, venison, pigeon, or boar depending on the land. The kill is clean, the meat will keep.
Sign of prey but never the prey itself — tracks leading nowhere, droppings cold, a half-glimpse through the undergrowth. Hours lost.
The prey is taken, but the meat is spoiled — diseased, tainted, wrong in some way. Herbalism may reveal the cause in time; if not, sickness follows.
events.on_eye: The spoilage is from Shadow corruption; 1 shadow point if any of the meat is eaten unknowing.
events.notes: Hazard event. Herbalism roll detects corruption before consumption.

events.label: A snare or deadfall fails badly — the device injures the hunter rather than the prey. A bruise, a gash, a sprained ankle — slow to recover.
events.notes: Self-inflicted hazard. Healing roll may reduce fatigue; no rations gained.

events.label: The hunt brings the hunter face to face with another hunter — a predator. The prey they sought is already dead, and the killer has just seen them.
events.on_eye: The predator attacks before the hunter can retreat.
events.notes: Cross-reference to Predators data file. Avoidance possible with Stealth; combat is the worst outcome.

foraging.label: Foraging
foraging.description: The hunter searches for plants, roots, fruit, mushrooms.

events.label: A good foraging find — a patch of wild garlic, a stand of fruiting berries, a mushroom-rich hollow. Enough for a meal or two.
events.notes: Yields 1d3 rations. Herbalism or Lore ensures safety of identification.

events.label: A genuine bounty — fruit trees heavy in season, a nut-tree scattering its crop, the forest generous. More than one meal, if the fellowship has means to carry it.
events.notes: Yields 1d6 rations; carrying capacity may limit.

events.label: Plants that might be food and might be poison — the hunter's knowledge is uncertain. Herbalism roll decides.
events.notes: Risk event. Herbalism success yields rations safely; failure yields sickness.

events.label: The land is bare here — the season is wrong, the ground already picked over, or the forest just not yielding. Hours spent, nothing gained.

stash.label: Hunting Stash
stash.description: The hunter finds a cache left by another — a hidden larder or hunter's store.

events.label: A hunter's stash, intact — dried venison, salt pork, smoked rabbit. Left by someone who expected to return; they clearly have not.
events.notes: Yields 1d6 rations. Moral question about taking another's food; likely academic if the owner is long gone.

events.label: A stash found too late — the food is spoiled, the wrappings rotted. The care that made the cache is still visible; the contents are beyond use.
events.notes: Nothing gained, but a small Lore roll may identify whose stash it was.

events.label: A stash with an unwelcome guardian — a wolverine, a bear, a nest of weasels already in residence and unwilling to share.
events.on_eye: The guardian is a predator; roll one from the Predators data file.
events.notes: Encounter event. Negotiation with wildlife is limited.

traps.label: Old Traps
traps.description: The hunter finds rabbit-snares or deadfalls set by another — still working, still yielding.

events.label: Working traps, yielding small game — rabbit, bird, a coney. The trapper's work; the hunter takes what is caught.
events.notes: Yields 1d3 rations. Some traditions hold it is fair to take from untended traps; others, theft.

events.label: A local trapper arrives as the hunter is inspecting their traps — they are not pleased. Negotiation or flight.
events.notes: Social event. Apology and news-for-traps may still yield food.

events.label: All the traps are sprung but empty — something has taken the catches already, perhaps a fox, perhaps a person. The trapper will be unhappy.
events.notes: Nothing gained; a moment of atmospheric mystery.
